user_query:
I’m building a weekly sourcing and compliance workflow for my sustainable seafood market business plan. Please set up a self-contained scheduled job that I can run locally every Monday at 09:00, and generate a sample run using a fixed date so we can verify the outputs.

Inputs (use these exact files):
- input/inventory.csv
- input/suppliers.csv
- input/regulations.html
- docs/business_plan.md (this must be edited in-place as described below)

What to build and produce:
1) A runnable script at scripts/run_weekly_update.sh that accepts a required --date YYYY-MM-DD argument and performs all processing below deterministically (no network use, no randomness). It must:
   - Parse input/inventory.csv and identify low-stock items where current_stock_kg < reorder_point_kg.
   - For each low-stock species, select the recommended sustainable supplier from input/suppliers.csv using all of these rules: matching species; certification must be MSC or ASC; sustainability_score >= 80; choose the lowest price_per_kg; if tied on price, choose the higher sustainability_score; if still tied, choose the supplier_name that comes first alphabetically.
   - Extract the “Requirement” values only from rows in input/regulations.html where the Category is “Retail” (ignore any other rows). Treat those as compliance notes.
   - Compose a weekly status report and draft an email as outputs (see required paths/structure below).
   - Edit docs/business_plan.md in-place by replacing only the content between the markers <!-- AUTO-UPDATE:SOURCING_START --> and <!-- AUTO-UPDATE:SOURCING_END --> with an auto-generated update that includes:
     • A Markdown table with columns exactly: Species | Current Stock (kg) | Reorder Point (kg) | Recommended Supplier | Price (per kg) | Certification. Include all low-stock species and the chosen supplier. If no eligible supplier exists, write “No eligible supplier” and leave Price and Certification blank.
     • A short bullet list titled “Compliance notes (Retail)” containing the extracted Retail requirements from input/regulations.html.

2) A cron-compatible schedule file at schedule/seafood_weekly.crontab containing a single line that runs every Monday at 09:00 local time and invokes the script with the current date. The line must include the timing fields “0 9 * * MON” and call scripts/run_weekly_update.sh with “--date $(date +%F)”. You do not need to install the cron entry; just create this file.

3) Run the workflow once locally as a verifiable dry run with the fixed date 2024-12-02 and produce these exact artifacts:
   - output/2024-12-02/weekly_status.md containing, in this order, the following top-level sections with these exact headings:
     • Low-stock items: a Markdown table listing each low-stock species with current_stock_kg and reorder_point_kg.
     • Recommended sustainable suppliers: a Markdown table with columns Species | Supplier | Price (per kg) | Certification that reflects the selection rules above.
     • Compliance notes: a bullet list of the Retail requirements extracted from input/regulations.html.
     • Data sources: a short list showing the row counts parsed from input/inventory.csv and input/suppliers.csv, and the count of Retail requirements extracted from input/regulations.html, so the results can be cross-validated.
   - docs/business_plan.md updated as described in (1). Ensure only the marked block is replaced.
   - output/2024-12-02/email_draft.txt with the first line exactly: Subject: Weekly sourcing & pricing update – 2024-12-02. Follow with a brief, clear message to my mentor summarizing: the number of low-stock species, the chosen sustainable supplier for each low-stock species with price, and a one-paragraph note on the key Retail compliance requirements extracted.

General constraints:
- Use only the provided inputs; do not fetch anything from the Internet.
- The script must be idempotent for the same --date and inputs (re-running with the same date should reproduce identical outputs).
- All paths above are relative to the workspace root. Keep all generated files under output/, schedule/, scripts/, or docs/ as specified.

Deliverables to leave in the workspace:
- scripts/run_weekly_update.sh
- schedule/seafood_weekly.crontab
- The dry-run outputs under output/2024-12-02/ as specified
- The edited docs/business_plan.md with the updated auto-generated block preserved between the markers. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
